titleInfo.title: Mianyang Beishan Yuan moya zaoxiang shulüe
綿陽北山院摩崖造像述略
A Brief Report on the Cliff Sculptures of Beishan Cloister in Mianyang

mods.abstract: Beishanyuan 北山院is located on Mount Bei 北山in the town of Weicheng 魏城镇in Youxian area 游仙区 of the city of Mianyang 绵阳市. The place is named after a temple with the same name that was built in 879 (Qianfu 6 乾符六年). This temple was also called Dafosi 大佛寺 because it had a large Maitreya statue. The rock carvings are distributed along a cliff stretching for 32 m, with the southern end being 5.1 m high and the northern end 2.8 m that is carved with 11 niches and one Diamondsutra. At a distance of 80 m from the cliff’s northern end, another boulder is located that is commonly called “Golden Cabinet” Jinguizi 金柜子. Niche 1 starts from the southern end of the cliff. The inscription next to the bodhisattva in niche 1 reads: “敬造弥勒大像一身 窃闻大像出现与日月之同明生□□⋯⋯普愿即坚千身万化灵力无□□□□□验获目前愚迷不辩有若干⋯⋯太乾符四年十月二日造佛舍谷⋯⋯八七七年(唐) , 黄□□, 五⋯⋯ 梓潼⋯⋯”。 Next to niche 6 with a three-headed figure is the following inscription: “⋯⋯建置北山院⋯⋯大唐乾符六年十二月十四日”。 The niche with the Diamondsutra carving is 3.85m wide, 0.82 m high, with each character being 3 cm high.
